pattern: A fast pointer moving twice as fast as a slow one, started two steps ahead, lands the slow pointer exactly one node before the middle just as the fast pointer runs off the end -- letting you unlink the middle node in a single pass with no length precount.
hints: You could count the list's length first and then walk to the middle, but a single pass with two pointers moving at different speeds gets there without ever knowing the length up front.
Start a slow pointer at head and a fast pointer two nodes ahead of it. Advance slow by one and fast by two each step; when fast runs out of room, slow is sitting just before the middle node.

Once slow is positioned right before the middle, unlink it with slow.next = slow.next.next. Handle the one- and two-node lists as special cases up front since there may be no 'node before the middle' to stand on.

edge_inputs.description: single node list, removing it empties the list
three-node list, middle is the exact center
five-node list with all equal values, position matters not value
six-node list, even length picks the later of the two center nodes
long uniform list to keep the two-pointer walk honest

stress.description: random-length lists with random values, spanning both odd and even lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def deleteMiddle(self, head):
        vals = []
        node = head
        while node:
            vals.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        n = len(vals)
        if n == 1:
            return None
        del vals[n // 2]
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        tail = dummy
        for v in vals:
            tail.next = ListNode(v)
            tail = tail.next
        return dummy.next
